title: From fintech frontends to AI agents: why this transition is coherent
excerpt: Production work in fintech shaped how I think about reliability, evaluation, and system boundaries. Those same constraints apply to agentic systems.
body:
I am not changing disciplines. I am extending the same engineering mindset into a new problem space.

Building financial systems taught me explicit failure handling, reproducibility, and clear boundaries between layers. Agentic systems need exactly that.

The transition becomes credible when you show real constraints, not only demos.

title: Evaluation-first development for LLM products
excerpt: Prompt changes without an evaluation loop are equivalent to deploying without tests. A practical baseline for safer iteration.
body:
An evaluation loop turns subjective output quality into measurable behavior.

At minimum, define representative scenarios, expected behavior, and clear pass or fail criteria.

Without this, prompt iteration is mostly guesswork.

title: What MCP changes for tool-enabled agents
excerpt: MCP standardizes how agents discover and invoke tools. The practical gain is composability and safer interfaces.
body:
Model Context Protocol reduces custom glue code between models and tools.

The architecture benefit is consistency in schemas, permissions, and invocation flow.

It does not remove complexity, but it makes complexity explicit and reusable.
